questions.title: What is the punishment for theft in Islam (under strict conditions)?

questions.title: The term 'Fard Kifayah' (Communal Obligation) is best exemplified by which of the following acts?
questions.answer: Participating in a funeral prayer (Salat al-Janaza)
questions.explanation:
Fard Kifayah (Communal Obligation) is a duty that is obligatory on the Muslim community as a whole.

The obligation is fulfilled if a sufficient number of individuals within that community carry it out.

If enough people perform the act, the rest of the community is absolved of the duty.

A prime example is the Salat al-Janaza (funeral prayer).

Once a group of Muslims performs the funeral prayer for the deceased, the obligation is met for the entire community.

questions.title: The first Islamic capital established outside the Arabian Peninsula was Kufa, founded during the early Caliphate in which modern-day country?
questions.explanation:
Kufa (Al-Kufah) was one of several amsar (garrison cities) founded by the Rashidun Caliphate outside the Arabian Peninsula.

It was established in Iraq in 638 AD by order of Caliph Umar Ibn al-Khattab.

questions.title: The Battle of Al-Qadisiyyah (636 AD) was the decisive Muslim victory that led to the collapse of which major empire?
questions.explanation:
The Battle of Al-Qadisiyyah (636 AD) was a pivotal confrontation between the forces of the Rashidun Caliphate and the Sasanian Empire.

Led by General Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, the Muslim army secured a decisive victory over the Persians, who were commanded by the legendary general Rostam Farrokhzad.

questions.title: The specific term for the part of a Hadith that contains the actual saying or action of the Prophet (PBUH) is ______?
questions.explanation:
A Hadith consists of two primary components.

The Matn is the actual body of the text, describing the specific words, actions, or silent approvals of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

It is the core message that conveys the religious or legal guidance.

questions.title: The philosophical concept of "Ijaz al-Qur'an" refers to the Qur'an's _____?
questions.explanation:
I'jaz al-Qur'an (Arabic: إعجاز القرآن) is an Islamic philosophical and theological concept that refers to the inimitability of the Quran.

It asserts that the Quran's content, style, linguistic beauty, structure, and depth are so unique and perfect that no human or group of humans can ever produce a text like it.

This quality is considered a form of divine miracle, serving as proof of its divine origin and the authenticity of the prophethood of Muhammad.

questions.title: The Islamic financial partnership known as 'Musharakah' involves profit and loss sharing, where capital is contributed by ____?
questions.explanation:
Musharakah (Arabic: مشاركة, meaning "sharing") is an essential concept in Islamic finance that represents a joint partnership or a joint venture.

It is a structure where all partners contribute capital, and they agree to share both the profits and any potential losses of the enterprise.

The key principles of Musharakah are based on equity and justice:

Profit Sharing: Profits are distributed among partners based on a pre-agreed ratio (which does not necessarily have to match the capital contribution ratio).

Loss Sharing: Losses, however, must be shared strictly in proportion to each partner's capital contribution.

questions.title: Which famous Muslim explorer and traveler covered nearly the entire Islamic world and parts of China and India in the 14th century?
questions.explanation:
Ibn Battuta (1304–1368) was a Moroccan scholar and explorer who is widely recognized as one of the greatest travelers in history.

Over a period of nearly 30 years starting in 1325.

He traveled approximately 75,000 miles (117,000 km).

He covering almost the entire Islamic world (Dar al-Islam) and beyond.

His journeys took him through North Africa, West Africa, Southern and Eastern Europe, the Middle East, India, Southeast Asia, and China.

His accounts were later compiled into a famous travelogue titled the Rihla.

questions.title: The famous Muslim historian and sociologist, known for his work Al-Muqaddimah, which laid the groundwork for modern historiography, was ______?
questions.explanation:
Ibn Khaldun (1332–1406) was a pioneering North African scholar often referred to as the father of modern sociology, historiography, and economics.

His most famous work, the Al-Muqaddimah (The Introduction).

It was written in 1377 as the preface to his planned history of the world.

questions.title: Who was the only Caliph to rule during the entire period of the Rashidun Caliphate (632-661 AD) without facing any internal civil wars?
questions.explanation:
Umar Ibn al-Khattab (634–644 AD) ruled during the Rashidun Caliphate without any internal civil wars.

His period is known for strong administration, justice, and stability within the Muslim state.

Major internal conflicts like the Ridda Wars occurred during Abu Bakr’s rule, and the First Fitna began in Uthman’s time—not during Umar’s caliphate.

questions.title: The legal principle of 'istihsan' (Juristic Preference), used when Qiyas (analogy) leads to an unfair result, is most closely associated with the methodology of which legal school?
questions.explanation:
Istihsan (Juristic Preference) is a key principle of the Hanafi school, used to avoid hardship or unfair outcomes.

It allows departure from strict Qiyas (analogy) when justice and public interest require it.

Imam Abu Hanifa emphasized istihsan to ensure flexibility and practicality in Islamic law.

questions.title: Which of the following is the Islamic philosophical term for the 'oneness of creation' or the unity of all existence?
questions.explanation:
Wahdat al-Wujud (Arabic: وحدة الوجود) literally means "Unity of Existence" or "Unity of Being".

This is a central philosophical concept in Islamic mysticism (Sufism) which posits a non-dualistic view where all existence is fundamentally one and a manifestation or expression of the Divine, meaning there is no true separation between the Creator (God) and the creation.

The perceived multiplicity of things is seen as different aspects or reflections of the singular ultimate reality, which is God.

questions.title: After the Battle of Hunayn, the Muslims established supremacy over which region?
questions.explanation:
The battle itself took place in the Hunayn valley, located in Western Arabia between Mecca and Taif. 

The Battle of Hunayn was fought against the Bedouin tribe of in 630 CE (8 AH)

It was fought between the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and his companions against the Hawazin and Thaqif tribes.

It took place shortly after the conquest of Makkah.

questions.title: The standardized Quranic text compiled under the caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan to resolve disputes is called?
questions.explanation:
The standardized Quranic text compiled under the caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan is known as the Uthmanic codex/ Mushaf Uthmani

The Uthmanic codex is the only edition of the Quran compiled by the third Rashidun caliph Uthman ibn Affan.

He ordered it to be copied and sent to Islamic countries after the death of Muhammad.

The Quran was collected in a single book by order of the first caliph Abu Bakr al-Siddiq.

questions.title: When does the time for Fajr prayer begin?
questions.explanation:
Fajr prayer time begins at Subh Sadiq (true dawn), when light first appears on the horizon, and ends at sunrise.

 Indicators of Fajr Time:

True Dawn (Al-Fajr Al-Sadiq):

  • A horizontal, widespread glow on the horizon that grows brighter.

False Dawn (Al-Fajr Al-Kadhib):

  • A vertical, fleeting light that appears before true dawn and is followed by darkness, which is not the start of Fajr time.

questions.title: How many types of Tawaf are there____________?
questions.explanation:
There are seven types of Tawaf;

1.      Tawaf-ul-Qudoom:

It is performed when a person arrived at Makkah whether for Hajj, Umrah, Business or a visit.

2.      Tawaf-ul-Ziarah:

It is also called Tawaf-ul-Rukn or Tawaf-ul-Hajj. It is performed by all pilgrims who are undertaking the Hajj. This Tawaf is mandatory

3.      Tawaf-ul-Wada (Tawaf-e-Sadr):

It is a farewell Tawaf and performed as a final ritual of Hajj when a pilgrim leaving Makkah.

4.      Tawaf-ul-Nafl:

Tawaf-ul-Nafl is an optional tawaf but it is better than performing a nafl salaat. It can be performed at any time and as desired.

5.      Tawaf-ul-Umrah:

Tawaf-ul-Umrah is a part of Umrah and performed as a ritual of Umrah. It is obligatory for the one who performs Umrah.

 

6.      Tawaf-ul-Nazr:

If a person pledged that he will perform Tawaf if his desire is fulfilled by Allah. Then, he must have to perform this Tawaf if Allah fulfills that desire.

 

7.      Tawaf-ul-Tahiyaah:

After Tawaf, the person has to offer two units behind Maqam-e-Ibrahim (if possible, otherwise they may offer that two units prayer anywhere in the mosque).

questions.title: Which surah is known as the Heart of Holy Quran?
questions.explanation:
Surah Yaseen is known as the "Heart of the Holy Quran."

It is the 36th chapter of the Quran.

It contains 83 verses.

It is regarded as an earlier Meccan surah.

Additional information:

Surah Ar-Rahman is known as "Aroos ul Quran" (the Bride of the Quran).

Other notable surahs include:

Al-Fatihah (the Opening)

Al-Baqarah (the Cow)

Aali Imran (the Family of Imran)

An-Nisa (The Women)

Al-Maidah (the Table)

questions.title: Ayat-ul-Kursi is verse number in Surah Baqrah is _____?
questions.explanation:
Ayatul Kursi is the 255th verse of the 2nd surah of the Quran, Al-Baqarah.

It is located in the third para named "تِلْكَ ٱلْرُّسُلُ."

It is also known as Sardar ul Ayat or The Throne Verse.

Surah Al-Baqarah is the longest Surah of the Qur'an.

It revealed after the Prophet ﷺ migrated to Madinah.

questions.title: Which surah is known to as Uroos ul Quran?
questions.explanation:
Surah Ar Rehman is called Uroos ul Quran.

It's the 55th Surah of the Holy Quran which has 78 verses in 3 Ruku. 

'Aroos-ul-Qur'an (the Bride of the Qur'an)

Surah Yusuf is called the father of the Quran.

Surah Yaseen is known as the heart of Quran.

questions.title: What is the name of religious book of Muslims?
questions.explanation:
The Quran is the holy book of Muslims.

It is believed to be the final revelation delivered to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

The Quran was revealed to Prophet Muhammad SAW.

The Injeel (Gospel) was revealed to Prophet Isa (Jesus).

The Torait (Tawrat/Torah) was revealed to Prophet Musa (Moses).

The Zuboor (Zabur/Psalms) was revealed to Prophet Dawud (David).

questions.title: Nafl is a/an _______ practice of worship.
questions.explanation:
Nafl refers to voluntary or optional acts of worship in Islam.

They are not compulsory, but bring extra reward when performed.

questions.title: Who is known by the title Umm Al-Arab (ام العرب)?
questions.explanation:
ام العرب (عربوں کی ماں) کا خطاب حضرت ہاجرہ علیہا السلام کو دیا جاتا ہے

یہ خطاب انہیں حضرت ابراہیم علیہ السلام کی زوجہ اور حضرت اسماعیل علیہ السلام کی والدہ ہونے کی وجہ سے دیا گیا ہے، جو تمام عرب قبائل کے جدِ امجد ہیں

اہمیت: اس لقب سے ان کی عرب تاریخ میں مرکزی حیثیت کا اندازہ ہوتا ہے. 

Don't be confused about two named 

Abu Al-Arab (ابوالعرب) — Prophet Ismail (حضرت اسماعیل)
Umm Al-Arab (ام العرب) — Hazrat Hajrah (حضرت حاجرہ)

questions.title: Who is known by the title Umm Al-Anbiya (ام الانبیاء)?
questions.explanation:
حضرت سارہؑ، حضرت ابراہیمؑ کی اہلیہ اور حضرت اسحاقؑ کی والدہ تھیں،

جنہیں اسلامی روایات میں "ام الانبیاء" (پیغمبروں کی ماں) کہا جاتا ہے

کیونکہ ان کی اولاد سے ایک پیغمبر یعنی حضرت یعقوبؑ کی نسل سے بہت سے انبیاء مبعوث ہوئے۔ 

اہمیت: حضرت سارہؑ کو اسلامی تاریخ میں "ام الانبیاء" کے لقب سے یاد کیا جاتا ہے کیونکہ ان کی نسل سے بہت سے انبیاء مبعوث ہوئے۔ 



Don't be confused about two named 

ابوالانبیاء (ابوالانبیاء): حضرت ابراہیمؑ

ام الانبیاء (ام الانبیاء): حضرت سارہؑ

questions.title: Who is known by the title Umm Al-Bashar (ام البشر)?
questions.explanation:
حضرت حواؑ کو "ام البشر" کہا جاتا ہے کیونکہ وہ تمام انسانوں کی ماں ہیں

حضرت آدمؑ کی بیوی ہیں. ان کا نام عربی میں "سرخ مائل" کے معنی دیتا ہے

نام کے معنی: "حوا" کا لفظی مطلب "سرخ مائل" ہے، اور ان کے ہونٹوں کی سفیدی کی وجہ سے یہ نام رکھا گیا ہے. 
Don't be confused about two named 
Abu Al-Bashar (ابوالبشر) — Prophet Adam (حضرت آدم)  
 Umm Al-Bashar (ام البشر) — Hazrat Hawwa (حضرت حواء)

questions.title: What did the Holy Prophet (SAW) abolish in Khutba Hujjat-ul-Wida?
questions.explanation:
In the Farewell Sermon, the Prophet (SAW) declared all blood feuds of the Jahiliyyah abolished.

This ended the cycle of tribal revenge and promoted peace and justice.

questions.title: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) at his last sermon ended which practice?
questions.explanation:
Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) abolished all pre-Islamic practices of usury (interest) and blood feuds/revenge killings

  • The Prophet (PBUH) delivered his final sermon during the Hajj on Mount Arafat.
  • It is also known as Farewell Sermon.
  • The Holy Prophet (SAW) performed Hajjat-ul-Wida in the 10th Hijri.
  • It was performed on Friday, the 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah (March 6, 632 A.D).
  • This final pilgrimage included his renowned Farewell Sermon.

questions.title: Which type of Hadith has numerous narrators at each level of the chain, making it impossible to be fabricated?
questions.explanation:
A Mutawatir Hadith is narrated by so many people at each stage that it is inconceivable they all agreed to lie.

This ensures its authenticity.

questions.title: A Hadith classified by the sequence of narrators is known as its?
questions.explanation:
Sanad (chain of narrators) refers to the sequence of people who transmitted the Hadith from the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) to the compiler.

The authenticity of a Hadith is verified by examining the reliability and continuity of its Sanad, along with the text (Matn).

questions.title: What is term for the hadith narrated by a single reliable narrator throughout the chain of transmission?
questions.explanation:
Gharib (غريب) is a term used in hadith studies to describe a hadith that is narrated by a single reliable narrator throughout the chain of transmission.

This means that the hadith is narrated by only one person at each level of the isnad (chain of transmission), making it a unique or solitary narration.

questions.title: Umm-ul-Masakeen (Mother of Poor) was the title given to one of the wives of the Prophet (SAW)?
questions.explanation:
Umm-ul-Masakeen was the title given to one of the wives of the Prophet (SAW): Hazrat Zainab benet Khuzima (RA)

Umm-ul-Masakeen means mother of the poor.

Hazrat Zainab benet Khuzima (RA) was the fifth wife of Muhammad

Zaynab was the first of Muhammad’s wives who were not from the Quraysh tribe.
 
Zaynab's marriage to Muhammad lasted only a few months.

She died at the age of about thirty.

Muhammad carried her to Jannat al-Baqi

questions.title: What is the khitab (title) of Hazrat Umar (RA)?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Umar (RA) was titled Al-Farooq, meaning the one who distinguishes between right and wrong.

This title reflects his justice, strength, and commitment to truth.

questions.title: The wife of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) who was the mother of his son Ibrahim was ______?
questions.explanation:
Maria Qibtiyya (RA) was gifted to the Prophet and gave birth to his son Ibrahim (RA).

Ibrahim (RA) passed away in infancy, but his birth is an important part of the Prophet’s life history.

questions.title: Bait-e- Rizwan was made to take the revenge of
questions.explanation:
Bait-e-Rizwan took place in 6th Hijrah.

Bait Rizwan was the pledge taken to get revenge of Hazrat Usman (RA) under the tree known as Rizwan

Bait-e-Rizwan is also known as Bait-e-Shajra made under the Keekar tree.
